Sync .beads/issues.jsonl to reflect progress on observation resume
sequencing, search sort extraction, and workflow resume hardening.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-dhdo","title":"Sync exported DB snapshots (immutable artifacts, versioned filenames)","description":"# Task: Sync exported DB snapshots (immutable artifacts)

## Goal
Make it easy to move **database snapshots** between machines for debugging or continuity, without ever touching the live DB file.

## Safety model
- Only sync snapshots created via `wa export` / `wa diag bundle` (explicit export paths).
- Never sync the live DB file or WAL/SHM.
- Treat snapshots as immutable artifacts:
  - versioned filenames
  - no in-place overwrite
  - optional compression (if/when supported by export)

## Naming & versioning
- Filenames should encode enough metadata to be self-describing:
  - wa version
  - created timestamp (UTC)
  - workspace key (hashed/short)
  - optional host

## Push/pull behavior
- `push` uploads snapshot(s) to the target under an artifacts directory.
- `pull` downloads snapshots and places them under a local artifacts directory.
- Default behavior is additive (no overwrites).

## UX expectations
- Plans should clearly distinguish:
  - “syncing snapshots” (safe)
  - “syncing live DB” (forbidden; should never appear)

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.8.5` (sync tests):
  - live DB paths are rejected
  - snapshot filenames are stable and never overwritten
  - push/pull round-trips via local-directory backend

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa sync push` can push an exported snapshot to a target without overwriting existing files.
- `wa sync pull` can pull the same snapshot back and preserves the filename.
- Attempts to sync live DB paths are rejected by the planner.

 {"id":"ft-djfxz","title":"Phase 4: Fix clippy errors in FrankenTerm crates","description":"Fix 8 clippy deny-by-default errors across FrankenTerm crates to achieve zero clippy errors workspace-wide.

## Fixes applied
1. wezterm-dynamic/src/error.rs: for-return → .into_iter().next() (never_loop)
2. wezterm-ssh/src/host.rs: for-break → .split_whitespace().next() (never_loop)
3. wezterm-ssh/src/sessioninner.rs: for-break → .split_whitespace().next() (never_loop)
4. config/src/lua.rs (2x): for-return → .sequence_values().next() (never_loop)
5. mux/src/tab.rs: for-return → .into_iter().next() (never_loop)
6. termwiz/src/caps/probed.rs (2x): .read() → .read_exact() (unused_io_amount)
7. termwiz/src/terminal/unix.rs: .write() → .write_all() (unused_io_amount)
8. wezterm-input-types/src/lib.rs: n >= 13 && n <= 35 → (13..=35).contains(&n) (manual_range_contains)
9. term/src/lib.rs: Added #![allow(clippy::unused_io_amount)] for 8 intentional write-discard patterns

## Why these aren't just warnings
FrankenTerm crates don't inherit workspace lints (no [lints] workspace = true). These are clippy's own deny-by-default lints (never_loop, unused_io_amount) which are treated as errors by cargo clippy.

-{"id":"ft-dp6sg","title":"[audit][T2.1] Make web server host/port configurable and deduplicate MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE","description":"# Make web server host/port configurable and deduplicate MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE

## Problem
web.rs line ~54 hard-codes DEFAULT_HOST="127.0.0.1" and DEFAULT_PORT=8000. Operators on shared machines get port conflicts. Operators wanting to expose the web API to a local network cannot without recompilation.

Additionally, main.rs defines MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE as 128KB at TWO separate locations (lines ~10162 and ~46389). These are independent constants that could drift apart, causing protocol mismatches between IPC paths.

## Fix
1. Move DEFAULT_HOST and DEFAULT_PORT to TuningConfig.web.default_host / default_port
2. Read from config in web server startup path
3. Deduplicate MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE: define it once in wire_protocol.rs (or tuning_config.rs) and import everywhere
4. Make it configurable: TuningConfig.ipc.max_message_size

## Files to modify
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/web.rs (replace const with config read)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/tuning_config.rs (add fields)
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs (remove duplicate const, import from core)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. ft.toml [tuning.web] default_host and default_port override the defaults
2. MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE defined in exactly one place
3. Existing behavior unchanged when no [tuning] section present
4. Tests verify config loading and that IPC/web use the same value","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:24.451606Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:37.822580Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","config","critical","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dp6sg","depends_on_id":"ft-fled9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:34.948842Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv","title":"[EPIC] Integrate /dp Rust tools into FrankenTerm — unified agent swarm platform","description":"# Integrate /dp Rust Tools into FrankenTerm

## Vision
FrankenTerm becomes the unified platform that harmonizes ALL of our Rust tools into a coherent agent swarm operating system. Instead of running dcg, cass, caut, tru, process_triage as standalone CLIs, they become native FrankenTerm subsystems — deeply integrated, automatically applied, zero-configuration.

## Methodology — MANDATORY SKILLS
1. **/idea-wizard** — Generate novel integration ideas and operationalize them into beads
2. **/extreme-software-optimization** — Profile integration overhead, ensure tools add <1% latency
3. **/alien-artifact-coding** — Formal verification for safety-critical integrations (dcg, process management)

## Tools to integrate (from /Users/jemanuel/projects/)

### Safety & Guard Rails
- **dcg** (destructive_command_guard) — Block dangerous commands before execution
- **process_triage** — Automated process lifecycle management (if exists, else build it)

### Intelligence & Context
- **cass** (coding_agent_session_search) — Search all past agent sessions for context
- **caut** (coding_agent_usage_tracker) — Track LLM usage/quotas across 16+ providers

### Serialization & Efficiency
- **tru** (toon_rust) — Token-optimized serialization (40-60% token savings vs JSON)

### TUI & Presentation
- **charmed_rust** — Elm-architecture TUI framework (bubbletea, lipgloss, bubbles)
- **frankentui** — Minimal high-performance TUI kernel with diff-based rendering
- **rich_rust** — Beautiful terminal output (tables, panels, progress bars)
- **opentui_rust** — Low-level TUI foundation

### Issue Tracking
- **br** (beads_rust) — Already integrated. Ensure deep hooks into FrankenTerm workflows.

## Integration philosophy
- **Library, not CLI**: Import as Rust crate dependencies, not subprocess calls
- **Zero-config**: Automatically enabled, no manual setup
- **Hot path safe**: Integrations must add <1% latency to pane operations
- **Graceful degradation**: If a tool's data isn't available, FrankenTerm works fine without it

## Success Criteria
- FrankenTerm integration plan remains feature-complete across all listed /dp Rust tools with no functional scope reduction.
- Each integration track defines explicit library-first contracts, migration strategy, and rollback safety for production-grade adoption.
- Validation requirements are comprehensive and enforceable: unit, integration, and end-to-end test scripts with detailed logging and diagnostics for each subsystem.
- Performance overhead budgets and observability standards are defined so integrations remain hot-path safe.
- Cross-tool cohesion is demonstrably improved for end users through coherent workflows, reduced operational friction, and consistent control surfaces.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Preserve full integration scope across all listed Rust tool tracks (including search, agent detection, safety, context, serialization, and TUI surfaces) with zero silent feature loss.
2. Require comprehensive validation coverage per active child track: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts for happy-path, edge-case, and failure/rollback workflows.
3. Require structured diagnostics/logging for all validation paths, including correlation identifiers, command context, timings, and artifact locations for deterministic triage.
4. Enforce performance and reliability budgets with measurable evidence (search latency, startup detection overhead, hot-path safety) and benchmark regression gates.
5. Close this epic only with auditable evidence bundles: executed commands (offloaded via rch for heavy runs), generated artifacts/logs, and residual risk notes.","notes":"Program sequencing gate: prioritize critical unblocks first (ft-dr6zv.1.3 for search lane, and ft-dr6zv.2.3 + ft-dr6zv.2.4 for agent-inventory lane), then converge on comprehensive integration suites (ft-dr6zv.1.7 and ft-dr6zv.2.5). Keep dependency graph cycle-free and treat any missing prerequisite as an explicit blocker bead before implementation proceeds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:11:53.569460Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T07:12:33.056965Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T07:12:33.056893Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv","depends_on_id":"ft-165vw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv","depends_on_id":"ft-2dss0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv","depends_on_id":"ft-2l9kn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv","depends_on_id":"ft-2zywl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv","depends_on_id":"ft-3hbv9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv","depends_on_id":"ft-a0owq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":861,"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Plan-space optimization pass completed on active ft-dr6zv integration beads (2026-02-22).

Scope covered:
- Root epic ft-dr6zv
- FrankenSearch track root + active tasks: ft-dr6zv.1, .1.3, .1.4, .1.5, .1.6, .1.7
- Agent-detection track root + active tasks: ft-dr6zv.2, .2.3, .2.4, .2.5

Revisions applied:
- Added explicit acceptance_criteria and notes for every active/blocked ft-dr6zv bead.
- Enforced unit/integration/e2e + structured logging/diagnostics expectations across all active beads.
- Added standardized labels (quality-gate, test-coverage, integration-tests, e2e-tests, logging, test-logging) to remove ambiguity in readiness filtering.
- Added sequencing guidance at root/track levels based on bv triage and plan outputs.
- Promoted ft-dr6zv.2.4 from P3 -> P2 for parity with ft-dr6zv.2.3 because both are prerequisites for ft-dr6zv.2.5 gate.

Validation snapshot (open + in_progress + blocked ft-dr6zv):
- total beads: 11
- missing acceptance_criteria: 0
- missing notes: 0
- missing test text: 0
- missing e2e text: 0
- missing logging/diagnostic text: 0

Diagnostic commands used:
- bv --robot-triage

Outcome: active dr6zv plan is now explicit, test-complete, observability-complete, and sequence-aware without reducing functionality.","created_at":"2026-02-22T17:47:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1","title":"Integrate FrankenSearch into FrankenTerm — replace DIY search with production library","description":"# Integrate FrankenSearch into FrankenTerm

## Vision
Replace FrankenTerm's current hand-rolled search module (`search/mod.rs` with its own embedder, chunking, hybrid_search, reranker) with the production-grade `frankensearch` library. FrankenSearch provides two-tier progressive delivery (fast results in ~15ms, quality-refined in ~150ms), FSVI vector format with f16 quantization and SIMD, Tantivy BM25, RRF fusion, and an embedder stack with automatic fallback — all battle-tested across three production codebases.

## Current State
FrankenTerm already has a `search/` module with its own hybrid search, chunking, embedder, and reranker code. This is a parallel implementation of what FrankenSearch does better and more robustly. The integration replaces the DIY version with the library.

## Key Integration Challenge
**Runtime mismatch**: FrankenSearch uses `asupersync` (structured concurrency, no Tokio), while FrankenTerm uses Tokio. A runtime bridge adapter is required. FrankenTerm already has an epic (`ft-brc7d`) for asupersync migration, so this integration should use feature-gated adapters that work with either runtime.

## Integration Surface
- Replace `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/` internals with frankensearch facade
- Wire `TwoTierSearcher` progressive callbacks into TUI search overlay
- Index terminal scrollback, session recordings, and agent output
- Expose via robot mode CLI and IPC status API
- Feature-gate behind `frankensearch` feature flag

## Success Criteria
- All existing search functionality preserved or improved
- Progressive delivery visible in TUI (fast results appear, then refine)
- Search latency p50 < 20ms for fast tier across 10K+ indexed documents
- Zero regression in pane rendering performance (search runs off hot path)
- Graceful degradation when search index unavailable

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Replace FrankenTerm search internals with frankensearch-backed implementations while preserving existing public API contracts and user-visible search workflows.
2. Deliver progressive two-tier behavior across TUI and robot mode (fast initial + refined follow-up) with no regressions in correctness or operator ergonomics.
3. Preserve/extend compatibility guarantees: feature-gated fallback behavior, backward-compatible output schemas, and deterministic rollback path for cutover incidents.
4. Require comprehensive test coverage for this track: unit tests, cross-module integration tests, end-to-end scripts, and benchmark/perf checks with explicit pass/fail thresholds.
5. Require structured logging/diagnostics and artifact capture for all validation runs (phase markers, latency breakdowns, error taxonomies, reproduction metadata).
6. Track closure requires command/artifact evidence and residual risk documentation for production readiness.","notes":"Critical-path sequencing for this track: ft-dr6zv.1.3 must complete first to unblock ft-dr6zv.1.4/1.5/1.6; after functional lanes settle, ft-dr6zv.1.7 serves as comprehensive validation gate. Any additional compat shim or fallback added during execution must include explicit retirement criteria to avoid permanent debt.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":1200,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:48:48.254684Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T07:12:25.567763Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T07:12:25.567702Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","frankensearch","integration","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","search","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","track-search","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.1.1","title":"Add frankensearch workspace dependency with feature gate","description":"## Context
FrankenSearch needs to be added as a workspace dependency and feature-gated in frankenterm-core so it can be optionally compiled.

## Scope
1. Add `frankensearch` to workspace `[dependencies]` in root Cargo.toml as a git dependency pointing to the frankensearch repo
2. Select appropriate feature set: start with `hybrid` (hash + model2vec + fastembed + lexical) for full two-tier capability
3. Add `frankensearch` feature flag to frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml gating the dependency
4. Add `frankensearch` to the default feature set (or a `full` preset) so it builds by default
5. Create `#[cfg(feature = "frankensearch")]` module declaration in lib.rs
6. Verify workspace builds clean with `cargo check --workspace`
7. Verify builds also work WITHOUT the feature: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features`

## Key Considerations
- frankensearch depends on `asupersync` — verify no version conflicts with any existing asupersync references in the workspace (ft-e34d9 migration may have introduced asupersync already)
- frankensearch pulls in `tantivy` — check for conflicts with frankenterm's existing tantivy usage in `tantivy_ingest.rs`, `tantivy_policy.rs`, `tantivy_query.rs`. Goal: single tantivy version in Cargo.lock
- frankensearch uses Rust 2024 edition — verify frankenterm's toolchain is compatible (edition is per-crate, so this should work, but nightly features may be needed)
- Document the feature flag in Cargo.toml with inline comments explaining what it enables

## Testing Requirements
- `test_feature_flag_on`: `cargo check --workspace` passes with frankensearch feature enabled
- `test_feature_flag_off`: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` passes cleanly
- `test_no_duplicate_tantivy`: `cargo tree -d | grep tantivy` shows zero duplicates
- `test_no_duplicate_asupersync`: `cargo tree -d | grep asupersync` shows zero duplicates
- `test_cfg_module_accessible`: frankensearch types importable when feature enabled
- `test_cfg_module_absent`: frankensearch types NOT importable when feature disabled (no compile error, just absent)
- Run these checks as a CI script: `tests/ci/check_frankensearch_feature.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check --workspace` passes with frankensearch feature enabled
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` passes without it
- No duplicate tantivy versions in Cargo.lock
- No duplicate asupersync versions in Cargo.lock
- Feature flag documented in Cargo.toml with inline comments
- CI script validates both feature-on and feature-off builds","notes":"Implemented workspace-level FrankenSearch integration with feature gating and lockfile convergence:
- Root `Cargo.toml`: added `frankensearch` workspace dependency (git rev `3ad4326f...`, feature `hybrid`, `default-features = false`).
- Root `Cargo.toml`: added `[patch."https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensearch"]` pinning `asupersync` to `c7c15f63...` to avoid transitive drift.
- Root `Cargo.toml`: aligned workspace `tantivy` to `0.22` so frankenterm + frankensearch resolve to one tantivy major.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: added optional dep `frankensearch`, feature `frankensearch = ["dep:frankensearch"]`, and made it default-on (`default = ["frankensearch"]`) with inline comments.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs`: added `#[cfg(feature = "frankensearch")]` module wiring (`search_bridge`) as integration placeholder for follow-on runtime bridge bead (`ft-dr6zv.1.2`).

Validation (offloaded via `/Users/jemanuel/bin/rch10 exec -- ...`):
1) `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib` => PASS (exit=0)
2) `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features` => PASS (exit=0)
3) `cargo check --workspace` => PASS (exit=0)

Duplicate-version sanity:
- `rg "^name = "tantivy"$" Cargo.lock` => single entry
- `rg "^name = "asupersync"$" Cargo.lock` => single entry
- `cargo tree -d --workspace | rg "^tantivy v|^asupersync v"` => no matches

Known warnings from `frankenterm-core` (`unused_mut`/`dead_code`) are pre-existing and unchanged by this bead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonReef","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-16T04:51:43.101157Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-16T05:22:14.885940Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T05:20:34.566205Z","close_reason":"Completed: frankensearch workspace dependency + feature gate integrated and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","frankensearch","integration","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dr6zv.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-dr6zv.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
